_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**Coverage Determination and Registration**

LL97 does not require a separate producer or EPR-style registration. Coverage is determined automatically by reference to NYC Department of Finance (DOF) records: a building's gross floor area as recorded by DOF determines whether it is subject to LL97. The workspace must cross-reference every NYC facility address against the DOB Sustainability Laws Covered Buildings List (CBL, most recent edition published March 2026) to confirm coverage status and compliance pathway. The CBL is accessible via the NYC DOB website and is updated when owners submit approved disputes. Presence or absence on the CBL does not relieve a building owner of legal obligations; owners must verify the accuracy of all information used for compliance.

**Reporting Portal and Account Setup**

Compliance reporting is processed through three linked systems: (1) a DOB NOW account (used to pay filing fees), (2) an EPA Energy Star Portfolio Manager (ESPM) account (used to input annual energy and water consumption data by fuel type, and to share data with NYC), and (3) the NYC Building Energy Analysis Manager (BEAM) portal at nyc.beam-portal.org (used for the official GHG emissions report submission and certified by an RDP). All three accounts must be established under the same credentials so they can be linked. As of 2026, all covered buildings must report using ESPM property types — the earlier option of using NYC Building Code occupancy groups expired after 2025.

**Data Fields the Workspace Must Collect**

For each covered BIN, the workspace (or its RDP) must collect: 12 months of utility bills for all fuel types (electricity in kWh, natural gas in kBtu, fuel oil in gallons, district steam in MMBtu); gross floor area by ESPM property type; building identification number (BIN) and borough-block-and-lot (BBL); and documentation of any adjustments (e.g., affordable housing, healthcare, shared energy service, non-standard fuels). Carbon coefficients are applied per fuel type — for 2024–2029: electricity at 0.000288962 tCO2e/kWh, natural gas at 0.00005311 tCO2e/kBtu.

**Reporting Scope and Format**

Annual reports reflect the prior calendar year's emissions (e.g., 2026 report covers 2025 data). Reports must be certified by a licensed engineer (PE) or registered architect (RA). For buildings with shared energy service or multiple buildings on a BBL, site plan diagrams showing all buildings, meter locations, and fuel types must be uploaded to BEAM. Questions and CBL disputes are directed to BEAM_LL97@buildings.nyc.gov.

*Source: NYC DOB LL97 Page, NYC DOB, March 2026. https://www.nyc.gov/site/buildings/codes/ll97-greenhouse-gas-emissions-reductions.page; NYC Accelerator LL97 Page, NYC Accelerator, 2026. https://accelerator.nyc/ll97; NYC DOB LL97 Compliance Report Submission Process, NYC DOB, May 2025. https://www.nyc.gov/assets/buildings/pdf/ll97-compliance-report-process.pdf*
